product_updates.title: 15% of Americans say they’d be willing to work for an AI boss, according to new poll
product_updates.content: According to a Quinnipiac University poll, 15% of Americans say they'd be willing to have a job where their direct supervisor was an AI program that assigned tasks and set schedules.

product_updates.title: Popular AI gateway startup LiteLLM ditches controversial startup Delve
product_updates.content: LiteLLM had obtained two security compliance certifications via Delve and fell victim to some horrific credential-stealing malware last week.

product_updates.title: As more Americans adopt AI tools, fewer say they can trust the results
product_updates.content: AI adoption is rising in the U.S., but trust remains low, with most Americans concerned about transparency, regulation, and the technology’s broader societal impact, according to a new Quinnipiac poll.

product_updates.title: Mantis Biotech is making ‘digital twins’ of humans to help solve medicine’s data availability problem
product_updates.content: Mantis takes disparate sources of data to make synthetic datasets that can be used to build so-called "digital twins" of the human body, representing anatomy, physiology and behavior.

product_updates.title: ScaleOps raises $130M to improve computing efficiency amid AI demand
product_updates.content: ScaleOps just raised $130M to tackle GPU shortages and soaring AI cloud costs by automating infrastructure in real time.

product_updates.title: AI chip startup Rebellions raises $400 million at $2.3B valuation in pre-IPO round
product_updates.content: The startup, which is planning to go public later this year, designs chips specifically for AI inference, another challenger to Nvidia's dominance.

product_updates.title: Mistral AI raises $830M in debt to set up a data center near Paris
product_updates.content: Mistral aims to start operating the data center by the second quarter of 2026.

product_updates.title: Qodo raises $70M for code verification as AI coding scales
product_updates.content: As AI floods software development with code, Qodo is betting the real challenge is making sure it actually works.

product_updates.title: Starcloud raises $170 million Series A to build data centers in space
product_updates.content: Starcloud becomes the fastest Y Combinator startup to reach unicorn status, just 17 months after demo day.

product_updates.title: Why OpenAI really shut down Sora
product_updates.content: OpenAI's decision last week to shut down Sora, its AI video-generation tool, just six months after releasing it to the public, raised immediate suspicions. The app had invited users to upload their own faces — so was this some kind of elaborate data grab?

technical_breakthroughs.title: There are more AI health tools than ever—but how well do they work?
technical_breakthroughs.content: Earlier this month, Microsoft launched Copilot Health, a new space within its Copilot app where users will be able to connect their medical records and ask specific questions about their health. technical_breakthroughs.title: Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless human clones
technical_breakthroughs.content: After operating in secrecy for years, a startup company called R3 Bio, in Richmond, California, suddenly shared details about its work last week—saying it had raised money to create nonsentient monkey “organ sacks” as an alternative to animal testing.
